summary:
The agent acknowledges limited circumstances—such as new severe LAD stenosis or need for arterial grafts—in which redo CABG might provide symptom improvement or procedural advantage in select octogenarian patients; however, overall, due to significantly elevated postoperative risks including up to tripled mortality compared to initial surgery and poor outcomes among frail older adults, such interventions generally fail to deliver measurable survival gains. Hence, despite occasional exceptions, the consensus remains that these operations do not reliably benefit octogenarians.

The agent recognizes that specific indications like new severe LAD stenosis or arterial conduit needs can justify redo CABG in selected octogenarians but emphasizes that it comes with markedly increased mortality risk (up to 3-4× greater than primary CABG) and lacks broad survival benefit, especially given patient comorbidities and low life expectancies. Consequently, overall cardiovascular reoperation does not meaningfully improve outcomes in this population. 

\boxed{\text{B}}

---

(End of Round)
